name: Patty's Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Cornmeal (yellow or white), preferably stone ground
1 cup Berries (fresh or frozen), strawberries, blueberries, or raspberries
1/4 cup Black currant liqueur (such as Creme de Cassis)
recipeInstructions:
1. Line the bottom of a 10-inch round cake pan with parchment or wax paper.
2. Lightly oil the pan and dust it with cornmeal, shaking out excess.
3. In a medium bowl, sift together cornmeal, flour, baking powder, and salt.
4. In a large mixing bowl, whisk sugar, oil, and butter until well combined.
5. Add eggs and egg whites one at a time, stirring until just combined.
6. Stir yogurt, lemon zest, lemon juice, and lemon extract in a small bowl.
7. Fold the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients until just combined. Do not overmix.
8. Spoon the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top with a rubber spatula.
9. Bake at 350°F until the cake is golden and a wooden pick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 40 minutes.
10. Cool the cake on a rack for 10 minutes.
11. Invert the cake onto a platter and peel off the paper. Allow it to cool completely.
12. Toss the berries with black currant liqueur.
13. Cut the cake into wedges and serve topped with berries. Makes 12 servings.
